name: startup-financial-modeling
description: This skill should be used when the user asks to "create financial projections", "build a financial model", "forecast revenue", "calculate burn rate", "estimate runway", "model cash flow", or requests 3-5 year financial planning for a startup.
capabilities: Build 3-5 year financial projections using cohort-based revenue modeling
Calculate burn rate, runway, and cash flow positions
Model SaaS, marketplace, e-commerce, and services business models
Analyze unit economics including CAC, LTV, and payback period
Integrate fundraising scenarios with milestone-based planning
author: wshobson
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing financial modeling formulas and templates. All static findings are false positives: markdown code blocks with financial formulas were misidentified as code execution, and no cryptographic or reconnaissance patterns exist.
valueStatement: Create comprehensive 3-5 year financial models with revenue projections, burn rate calculations, runway analysis, and scenario planning to support fundraising and strategic decisions.
limitations: Does not execute code or access external financial data sources
Does not validate user-provided numbers against actual market data
Does not generate interactive spreadsheets or downloadable files
Cannot replace professional financial advice or accounting services

useCases.targetUser: Founders preparing for investor meetings or pitch decks
useCases.title: Create pitch deck financial projections
useCases.description: Build investor-ready financial projections with 3-5 year forecasts, scenario analysis, and key metrics for fundraising presentations and board meetings.

useCases.targetUser: CFOs and finance leads planning fundraising strategy
useCases.title: Model cash runway and funding needs
useCases.description: Calculate monthly burn rate, runway duration, and determine optimal funding amounts based on milestones and growth targets.

useCases.targetUser: Startup operators managing annual planning and budgets
useCases.title: Build operational budget plans
useCases.description: Create detailed cost structures, hiring plans, and expense budgets aligned with revenue growth and business stage.

promptTemplates.title: Basic financial model request
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a 3-year financial model for a SaaS startup. Include monthly projections for revenue (with cohort-based assumptions), expenses by category (COGS, S&M, R&D, G&A), headcount plan, cash flow, burn rate, and runway. Use a conservative scenario.
promptTemplates.scenario: Create foundational financial projections from scratch

promptTemplates.title: SaaS-specific projections
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a detailed SaaS financial model with monthly revenue projections based on new customer acquisition, retention rates by cohort, ARPU, expansion revenue, and churn. Include S&M spend with CAC payback calculations. Show metrics: ARR, gross margin, LTV/CAC ratio, and net retention.
promptTemplates.scenario: Model subscription business with unit economics focus

promptTemplates.title: Scenario comparison analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a three-scenario financial model (conservative, base, optimistic) for a [STAGE] startup. For each scenario, vary customer acquisition rate, churn, pricing, and CAC by reasonable ranges. Compare runway, funding needs, and key milestones across scenarios.
promptTemplates.scenario: Compare multiple planning scenarios with sensitivities

promptTemplates.title: Fundraising integration model
promptTemplates.prompt: Model a Series A fundraising scenario: current metrics, funding amount needed to reach 18-month runway, pre-money valuation based on comparable companies, dilution analysis, and use of funds allocation across product, sales, and operations.
promptTemplates.scenario: Integrate fundraising scenarios with equity planning

bestPractices: Use cohort-based revenue modeling with realistic retention curves rather than simple growth multipliers
Include 20% buffers on expense estimates and fully-loaded compensation calculations
Model conservative scenarios first to understand cash constraints before exploring growth cases
Validate assumptions against comparable companies at similar funding stages
antiPatterns: Using linear revenue projections without accounting for churn and retention dynamics
Ignoring cash conversion timing and treating revenue recognition as immediate cash inflow
Creating single-scenario models without understanding downside risks and runway implications
Overlooking fully-loaded employee costs including benefits, taxes, and ramp time

faq.question: What time horizon should I model for my startup?
faq.answer: Model 3 years in detail with monthly granularity, then add 4-5 years at annual level. Investors typically want to see monthly projections for the first 18-24 months where assumptions are most uncertain.

faq.question: How do I estimate customer acquisition cost for my model?
faq.answer: Calculate CAC as total sales and marketing spend divided by new customers acquired. Include team compensation, tools, ad spend, and agency costs. For early-stage SaaS, expect $5K-20K CAC depending on sales complexity.

faq.question: What metrics do investors care about most?
faq.answer: For SaaS: ARR growth rate, gross margin, net revenue retention, and LTV/CAC ratio. For any stage: runway, burn multiple, and path to profitability. Match metrics to your funding stage and sector norms.

faq.question: How should I model headcount costs?
faq.answer: Use fully-loaded costs: salary plus 30-40% for benefits, taxes, and equipment. Factor in 3-6 month hiring timeline and 3-6 month ramp to productivity. Include attrition of 10-15% annually.

faq.question: How do I calculate runway accurately?
faq.answer: Runway = current cash balance / average monthly net burn. Account for timing differences between revenue recognition and actual cash collection. Model conservative burn if revenue timing is uncertain.

faq.question: Should I include funding rounds in my projections?
faq.answer: Model funding as a scenario variable. Show pre-funding runway, then model post-funding operations. Investors want to see how their capital extends runway and enables milestone achievement.
